mentions.occurrence_sentence: However, scaling this approach introduces systemic challenges. While technological capability exists, adoption is often constrained by fragmented funding, slow acquisition pathways, legacy infrastructure and limited compute availability at the tactical edge. Without changes to delivery models, even mature technologies risk underutilization. ## Why Legacy Investment Models Are Misaligned Many defense investment frameworks remain structured around hardware delivery. Budgeting and procurement processes continue to prioritize physical systems, with software and digital infrastructure treated as secondary considerations. This creates a mismatch between development cycles. Hardware operates on multi-year timelines, while software evolves continuously. Applying legacy acquisition models to software-defined systems introduces delays and reduces responsiveness. Cultural factors further reinforce this misalignment. Software teams are often positioned as support functions rather than core contributors to mission capability. This limits integration across engineering, operations and decision-making layers.
